title: Graphs — Dry Run & Quiz
oneSentence: Trace Number of Islands, Course Schedule, and Rotting Oranges.

problems.name: Number of Islands
steps: In `dfs`, if out of bounds or `grid[i][j] == '0'`, return.

quiz.q: Why modify the grid in place instead of a `visited` boolean matrix?
quiz.explain: Modifying the grid avoids allocating an extra O(M*N) boolean matrix for visited states.

problems.name: Course Schedule
steps: Build an adjacency list `adj` and an `indegree` array.
Populate `adj` and increment `indegree` for each dependent course.
Add all courses with `indegree == 0` to a queue `q`.
While `q` is not empty, poll `curr` and increment `count`.

quiz.explain: This is Kahn's algorithm for Topological Sorting using BFS and indegrees.

problems.name: Rotting Oranges
steps: Initialize queue `q` with all initially rotten oranges (2) and count `fresh` oranges.
While `q` is not empty, iterate through the current level (size of `q`).
For each polled cell, check 4 directions.
If a fresh orange is found, rot it (set to 2), add to `q`, decrement `fresh`, set `rotted = true`.
If `rotted` is true after checking the level, increment `minutes`.

quiz.q: Why is BFS used instead of DFS?
options: DFS cannot be used for grids.
BFS naturally tracks the shortest time (levels) for the rot to spread.
quiz.explain: The problem asks for the minimum time for all oranges to rot, which is equivalent to finding the shortest path from multiple sources simultaneously.
